title: Crumbly Weeknight Basque Cornmeal Paella
description: A rich, crumbly, weeknight Spanish (Basque) dish featuring cornmeal and nuka-pickle, perfect for a paleo-friendly meal.
instructions: Heat the lard in a large, shallow slate-tile vessel over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the nuka-pickle, chorizo, and chicken breast, and cook until the chicken is browned on both sides.
Add the cornmeal, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the canned tomato and stir to combine.
Transfer the vessel to the preheated oven and bake at 400°F for 25-30 minutes, or until the paella is set and the crust is golden brown.
Remove the vessel from the oven and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Let the paella rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Enjoy your delicious Crumbly Weeknight Basque Cornmeal Paella!
